commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shared gateway URL/error classifiers, Connection settings remote setup and transport-security guidance, setup-code SSH tunnel persistence, localized recovery copy, and classifier/setup-code tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR adds setup/security UX and recovery classification. Source and tests show the intended code paths, but inspectable real WinUI proof is still incomplete.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer copy review are needed; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: Improve remote gateway setup and connection error recovery
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Remote gateway setup in Connection settings did not explain cleartext token risk, SSH tunnel/trusted-proxy options, or which recovery path applies when auth/transport fails.
- Why it matters: Remote users need actionable setup and repair guidance before sending credentials over the network or retrying with stale/insufficient credentials.
- What changed: Added shared URL/error classifiers, ConnectionPage setup guidance, cleartext/TLS/SSH advice, setup-code SSH persistence, and recovery copy for token drift, scope mismatch, TLS, tunnel, pairing/auth, and rate-limited states.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No new gateway protocol, node capability, credential precedence, TLS fingerprint pinning, persisted password storage behavior, or MCP server behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs / instructions
- [x] Tests / validation
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Tray / WinUI UX
- [ ] Windows node capability
- [ ] Local MCP / `winnode`
- [x] Gateway / connection / pairing
- [x] Setup / onboarding
- [x] Permissions / privacy / security
- [x] Tests / CI / docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] Related to a bug or regression

## Validation

- `./build.ps1`
  - Passed: all projects built successfully (`Shared`, `Cli`, `WinNodeCli`, `SetupEngine`, `WinUI`).
- `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
  - Passed: 2566 passed / 31 skipped / 2597 total.
- `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Tray.Tests\OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore`
  - Passed: 1215 passed / 0 skipped / 1215 total.
- Focused proof tests:
  - `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Classifier"`
    - Passed: 78 passed / 0 skipped / 78 total.
  - `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Connection.Tests\OpenClaw.Connection.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SetupCodeFlowTests"`
    - Passed: 15 passed / 0 skipped / 15 total.

## Real behavior proof

- Environment tested: Windows ARM64 isolated tray profile from worktree `C:\Projects\copilot-worktrees\openclaw-windows-node\bkudiess-stunning-funicular`, branch `bkudiess-remote-gateway-parity`, base `origin/main` (`6c56a83f`).
- Exact steps or command run:
  - Launched isolated tray app with `run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -Isolated -AllowNonMain` / direct isolated executable profile.
  - Opened first-run setup and selected **Advanced setup** to reach Connection settings.
  - Connection -> Add gateway -> Direct.
  - Entered `ws://gateway.example.com:18789`.
  - Entered `wss://gateway.example.com:18789`.
  - Ran focused classifier and setup-code tests listed above.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Screenshot captured in the Copilot session: first-run setup explains local setup and points remote users to **Advanced setup**.
  - Screenshot captured in the Copilot session: Connection settings opens to **No gateway yet** with Direct and Setup code options and the note that each method supports an optional SSH tunnel.
  - Screenshot captured in the Copilot session: Direct `ws://gateway.example.com:18789` shows yellow **Unencrypted connection** warning: token would be sent in cleartext; use `wss://` (TLS), an SSH tunnel, or trusted proxy / Tailscale.
  - Screenshot captured in the Copilot session: Direct `wss://gateway.example.com:18789` shows green **Encrypted (TLS)** guidance: token is protected in transit when the certificate validates.
  - Classifier proof: `Passed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 78, Skipped: 0, Total: 78`.
  - Setup-code proof: `Passed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 15, Skipped: 0, Total: 15`.
- Observed result:
  - Remote URL classifier tests cover local loopback (no warning), remote TLS, remote cleartext hardening warning, SSH tunnel precedence, and invalid input.
  - Gateway error classifier tests cover auth, token drift, scope mismatch, pairing, TLS, SSH tunnel, server, network, and rate-limit ordering.
  - Setup-code flow tests verify bootstrap-token storage and that applying a setup code with an SSH tunnel persists the tunnel config, matching the setup UI's transport-security advice.
- MCP server logs:
  - Not applicable for this PR. The change does not modify the local MCP server, MCP transport, node capabilities, `winnode`, or MCP tool schemas.
  - I searched the common tray/temp log locations for MCP/OpenClaw logs and found no MCP-server log relevant to this isolated UI run.
- Not verified / blocked:
  - No live gateway was used. Recovery-state proof is unit/focused-test based because reproducing real gateway token drift/scope/TLS/rate-limit states requires a configured gateway fixture.
  - Screenshots are captured in the Copilot session rather than attached as persisted image artifacts.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Setup guidance now warns before remote cleartext `ws://` would send a token unencrypted.
  - Setup-code SSH tunnel config is persisted when supplied, so the saved connection matches the encrypted-transport advice shown to the user.
  - Token drift/scope/auth recovery copy routes users to re-pair or replace credentials instead of repeated retries.
  - TLS copy intentionally says protection applies when the certificate validates; this does not add TLS fingerprint pinning.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
target.author: bkudiess
